description: On / off toggle. Radix renders a <button role="switch"> with aria-checked reflecting the current state. The visual thumb slides via data-[state=checked]:translate-x-4. Unlike Checkbox, there is no indeterminate state.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the switch.

focus.notes: Uses focus-visible:ring-2 with ring-offset-2. Disabled switches show cursor-not-allowed and opacity-50 but remain in the tab order unless tabIndex={-1} is added.

announcements: Announces as "switch" with its accessible name and current on / off state.
screenReader.notes: Switch has no visible label of its own. Always pair with a <Label htmlFor> or wrap in a <label>, or provide aria-label / aria-labelledby. For settings with an immediate effect, Switch is usually the right choice over Checkbox because users perceive it as a live toggle.

contrast.ui: Checked state uses bg-primary; unchecked uses bg-input. Thumb uses bg-background. All meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Thumb uses transition-transform. Gate with motion-reduce:transition-none if you need strict reduced-motion compliance; the state is still conveyed by position + color.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide an accessible name via a linked <Label> or aria-label; the switch alone has none.
Use Switch for settings that take effect immediately (autoSave, notifications). Use Checkbox for form fields that require a Submit.
Don't rely on color alone to convey state; Radix already uses position (thumb left / right) as a secondary indicator, which works for color-blind users.
